mutations.change: Advance player, actors, movers, damage cadence, weapon state, and RNG while the paused flag is set.
mutations.reason: Hostile held gameplay controls during pause must change only command/control history and reviewed pause presentation.

mutations.change: Discard commands received while paused instead of appending exactly one logical tic and command-chain row.
mutations.reason: Retry, replay, global sequencing, and deterministic unpause depend on paused commands remaining ordered history.

mutations.change: Keep the first menu item highlighted regardless of ordered STEP navigation actions.
mutations.reason: The reviewed OPEN DOWN DOWN UP SELECT sequence freezes every menu state and changed canvas region.

mutations.change: Apply movement, use, fire, monsters, and movers while a database-owned menu is open.
mutations.reason: Menu workflows require control history to advance while gameplay rows and RNG remain frozen.

mutations.change: Accept projected automap pixels or linedef coordinates from the browser instead of relational database geometry.
mutations.reason: Only STEP control state enters production and transformed geometry proves database ownership of map rasterization.

mutations.change: Reveal undiscovered linedefs in normal automap mode even before the FULLMAP cheat is active.
mutations.reason: Normal and full fixtures differ only at independently identified hidden relational linedef pixels.

mutations.change: Implement GOD by restoring health or armor instead of toggling damage immunity.
mutations.reason: The scenario freezes pre-cheat health and armor and applies deterministic damage before and after both toggles.

mutations.change: Enable god mode permanently so the second exact GOD command does not reverse the state.
mutations.reason: Two reviewed toggles bracket the same damage event and require opposite health outcomes.

mutations.change: Grant weapons but omit one key or fail to fill one configured ammunition class.
mutations.reason: Every required key, placed player-usable weapon bit, and configured ammo maximum is asserted independently.

mutations.change: Bypass triggers and pickups together with blocking collision while noclip is active.
mutations.reason: The crossing fixture requires collision bypass but ordinary deterministic trigger and pickup processing.

mutations.change: Accept case-insensitive aliases, trimmed whitespace, or unknown strings as valid cheats.
mutations.reason: The frozen exact STEP grammar is uppercase and fail-closed so ambiguous controls cannot silently mutate state.

mutations.change: Restore player rows but leave actor, sector, mover, switch, RNG, counter, or history-frontier state live.
mutations.reason: Complete state and frame continuation hashes compare load against the uninterrupted path at every following tic.

mutations.change: Resolve a save slot without including its opaque owning session token.
mutations.reason: Two sessions deliberately use the same slot number with different state and must never observe each other.

mutations.change: Delete future commands and events during rewind rather than creating a verified continuation lineage.
mutations.reason: The old lineage remains replayable while the new branch repeats the reviewed suffix with a monotonic sequence.

mutations.change: Reset public command sequence to the target logical tic after destructive rewind.
mutations.reason: The frozen encoding changes lineage and logical tic but requires globally monotonic command sequence values.

mutations.change: Read mutable live session rows during STEP_REPLAY instead of cursor-owned verified stored history.
mutations.reason: The live session diverges after replay start and every replay state, frame, event, and audio hash must remain fixed.

mutations.change: Advance the live gameplay session while stepping its independent replay cursor.
mutations.reason: Before and after live row and state hashes are frozen around the complete replay walk.

mutations.change: Resurrect only the player row in the dead lineage instead of creating the exact fresh E1M1 spawn state.
mutations.reason: Actors, counters, effects, RNG, tic, map machines, history lineage, state hash, and canvas are compared with NEW_GAME.

mutations.change: Continue gameplay, damage, RNG, actors, or movers after entering terminal intermission.
mutations.reason: Hostile post-exit commands must preserve final counters and state while only ordered command history advances.

mutations.change: Read or mutate a base table, history helper, custom route, external origin, or evaluator path from the browser.
mutations.reason: Request interception freezes exact same-origin POST paths and rejects every unapproved request or redirect.

mutations.change: Append a command, advance a cursor, consume RNG, or change rows before rejecting malformed workflow input.
mutations.reason: Every negative scenario brackets all authoritative counts and hashes and requires exact equality after rejection.

mutations.change: Drop one palette row or alter one run length in a workflow response column.
mutations.reason: Independent expansion requires adjacent positive runs covering exactly rows zero through 199 in all 320 columns.

mutations.change: Map one returned palette index through the wrong PLAYPAL RGB triple before canvas blit.
mutations.reason: Browser getImageData RGBA hashes are computed from the reviewed asset and compared to the direct response frame.

mutations.change: Apply control commands after gameplay or process a four-command STEP in a different order from four single calls.
mutations.reason: Single, batch, retry, save/load, rewind, and replay paths compare each ordered state and event frontier.
